commands.name: reload
commands.description: Rebuild the content library off-thread and hot-swap it in (or just validate).

commands.name: give
commands.description: Give any mintable item (book, scroll, dust, gem, orb, crystal, set piece, heroic…) to a player.

commands.name: enchant
commands.description: Apply an enchant to the held item (admin; bypasses apply rules).

commands.name: removeenchant
commands.description: Strip an enchant from the held item.

commands.name: unenchant
commands.description: Alias of /se removeenchant.

commands.name: crystal
commands.description: Mint a socketable crystal to yourself.

commands.name: heroic
commands.description: Mint a Heroic upgrade item to yourself.

commands.name: orb
commands.description: Mint a slot-expander orb to yourself.

commands.name: gem
commands.description: Mint a soul gem to yourself.

commands.name: book
commands.description: Mint an enchant book to yourself.

commands.name: blackscroll
commands.description: Mint a black scroll (extracts a random enchant from gear to a book).

commands.name: randomizer
commands.description: Mint a randomizer scroll (rerolls a book's success chance).

commands.name: transmog
commands.description: Mint a transmog scroll (re-skins an item's appearance).

commands.name: godlytransmog
commands.description: Mint a godly transmog tool (reorder an item's enchant lore).

commands.name: holy
commands.description: Mint a holy scroll (keeps items + levels on death).

commands.name: nametag
commands.description: Mint an item nametag.

commands.name: dust
commands.description: Mint success dust (a fixed percent, or a random roll).

commands.name: whitescroll
commands.description: Mint a white scroll (protects an item from a black scroll).

commands.name: unopened
commands.description: Mint an unopened book of a rarity tier.

commands.name: soulmode
commands.description: Toggle your active soul gem's soul mode.

commands.name: split
commands.description: Split souls off your active gem into a new gem.

commands.name: migrate
commands.description: Import EliteEnchantments / EliteArmor / AdvancedEnchantments configs into the unified schema.

commands.name: import
commands.description: Import an enchant from an SE1 code (e.g. from the web Enchant Creator) and reload.

commands.name: pack
commands.description: Manage config-pack ZIP snapshots of your whole setup.

commands.name: menu
commands.description: Open an in-game GUI (enchanter, alchemist, tinkerer, transmog, browsers).

commands.name: effects
commands.description: Browse the effect reference in chat.

commands.name: selectors
commands.description: Browse the selector reference in chat.

commands.name: triggers
commands.description: Browse the trigger reference in chat.

commands.name: conditions
commands.description: Browse the conditions reference in chat.

commands.name: variables
commands.description: Browse the condition variables in chat.

commands.name: list
commands.description: List every enchant, set, and crystal.

config:
# StarEnchants — master config (docs/v3-directives.md §L).
#
# This file holds the CROSS-CUTTING knobs: ceilings and switches that are not a single item's "physical
# likeness" (those live in items/*.yml) and not player-facing text (that lives in lang.yml). Edit here, then
# run /se reload — the master config, items/, menus/, lang.yml and content/ all reload together in one
# transaction. Most values below are re-read live on the next use, so a reload re-tunes them without a restart
# (the exceptions — integration discovery and the auto-reload interval — are armed once at boot and noted).

# Per-feature master switches. Each turns a whole subsystem on/off. enchants/sets/crystals/heroic drop that
# source when a player's worn state is resolved (their combat + trigger effects go inert; re-read live, so a
# /se reload takes effect at once). slots/souls/scrolls gate their apply/interaction listeners at boot, so a
# change to those three takes effect on the next server start (like the integration toggles below).
features:
  enchants: true   # enchant effects fire in combat / on triggers
  sets: true       # armour-set bonuses (and the held-weapon set bonus) apply
  crystals: true   # crystal effects fire
  heroic: true     # heroic flat stats feed the heroic damage stage
  slots: true      # the slot-expander orb can be applied to gear
  souls: true      # the soul gem system (deposit on kill, soul mode, gem inventory)
  scrolls: true    # scroll-family interactions (black/randomizer/transmog/holy/nametag/godly transmog)

# Combat caps (cross-cutting). The additive damage fold (ADR-0012) sums every source's outgoing% and
# reduction%; these ceil those sums. A negative value = no cap (the default — current behaviour). pvp/pve gate
# whether StarEnchants combat effects apply at all in that context. All read live on /se reload.
combat:
  max-bonus-damage: -1.0      # ceiling on summed outgoing-damage fraction (e.g. 5.0 = at most +500%); <0 = uncapped
  max-bonus-reduction: -1.0   # ceiling on summed damage-reduction fraction (e.g. 0.8 = at most 80%); <0 = uncapped
  pvp: true                   # apply combat effects in player-vs-player hits
  pve: true                   # apply combat effects in player-vs-environment hits

# Player feedback (chat style). prefix is prepended to every message the plugin sends through its message
# facade (chat feedback only — never item names/lore). feedback toggles the keyed gameplay-feedback channel
# (e.g. menu confirmations); false = a quieter server (admin command echoes still print). Read live on reload.
messages:
  prefix: ""        # e.g. "&8[&dSE&8] " — prepended to plugin chat messages
  feedback: true    # send keyed gameplay-feedback messages to players

# Enchant slots (§H). The hard cap on TOTAL slots (base + purchased) lives in the expander's own config
# (items/slots.yml -> hard-cap), per §H; only the base every item starts with is cross-cutting.
slots:
  base: 9

# Souls (§D). The full soul economy — per-kill amount, per-mob overrides, colour tiers, sounds, particles,
# messages — is the gem's likeness in items/soul-gem.yml. Only the master deposit switch lives here.
souls:
  # Whether souls deposit into a carried gem on ANY kill. false disables the deposit-on-kill mechanic entirely
  # (the give/combine/split commands and soul-cost spending still work).
  deposit-on-any-kill: true

# Crystals (§E). Per-item crystal slots are a SEPARATE ledger from enchant slots; the crystal ITEM's likeness
# (material/name/lore/chance) lives in items/crystal.yml.
crystals:
  # Crystal slots every item has by default.
  slots: 1
  # Absolute maximum crystals one item may ever hold (a PDC-bloat sanity guard, distinct from the slot count).
  max-stack: 16

# Heroic (§F / ADR-0021). The granted percents, success range and material-upgrade map are the upgrade's
# likeness in items/heroic.yml. Only the bounded-multiplicative CLAMP CEILING is cross-cutting: heroic applies
# as a distinct stage after the additive damage fold, outgoing x clamp(1 + sum, 0, max-outgoing-factor).
heroic:
  # The ceiling on the heroic outgoing-damage multiplier (>= 1.0). 4.0 = heroic can at most quadruple output.
  max-outgoing-factor: 4.0

# Lore render style (§L). Legacy '&' colour codes. Re-read on every lore render, so a reload restyles live.
lore:
  enchant-color: "&7"      # an enchant's display name
  level-color: "&f"        # the level numeral; leave BLANK ("") to make the level use the enchant's tier colour
  crystal-color: "&b"      # a crystal line
  roman: true              # levels as Roman numerals (Venom III) vs Arabic (Venom 3)
  unknown-label: "&8Unknown Enchant"  # shown for a stored key no longer in the catalog
  # Auto-wrap width (VISIBLE chars; colour codes don't count) for AUTHORED economy/identity item lore —
  # scrolls, the orb, dust, the soul gem, the trak gems, etc. Authors write one long line and it word-wraps
  # to this width (explicit line breaks stay hard breaks). 0 = off. The enchant book uses its OWN per-file
  # width (items/enchant-book.yml -> wrap) for its templated {DESCRIPTION}, so it is unaffected here.
  item-wrap: 30

# Integration discovery (§N). Toggles read ONCE at boot (un-discovering a provider mid-run is not clean), so a
# change here takes effect on the next server start. Named integrations (worldguard, vault, mental, …) read
# from the 'named' map below; an unlisted name is enabled. e.g. `named: {mental: false}` stops StarEnchants
# coordinating KNOCKBACK_CONTROL with the Mental knockback plugin.
integrations:
  protection: true   # discover ProtectionProviders for the region/build gate
  economy: true      # discover an EconomyProvider for the money effects
  named: {}

# Reload behaviour (§L).
reload:
  re-resolve-players: true  # re-resolve every online player's worn state after a content swap
  auto-seconds: 0           # auto-/se-reload interval in seconds (0 = off; armed once at boot)

# Command trigger (§B). The COMMAND trigger fires a player's worn COMMAND-trigger enchants when they run a
# configured command. Registered ONCE at boot (a command name cannot be re-bound mid-run cleanly), so a
# change here takes effect on the next server start, not a /se reload.
command-trigger:
  enabled: true                                     # register the command at boot
  name: cast                                        # the command players run (no leading slash): /cast
  description: "Trigger your COMMAND enchantments."  # shown in tab/help listings
